spike_statement: The seafloor climbs from a thousand feet of open Pacific to about 51 cm of coral in a few hundred yards, with no shelf to soften it — so the swell doesn't shoal, it folds, into the heaviest rideable wave on Earth.

levers.photo_tone: a thick turquoise slab of water folding over a shallow coral reef, the green Tahiti Iti ridge behind, the deep-blue channel alongside, hard tropical Pacific light
